About BOS

My name is William, the founder of Bill of Senga (BOS). I turned a 'tired' piece of land into a productive one through natural way and that is the foundation of what I do today.

Welcome to this living platform — a space for practical ideas and real-world insights on sustainable farming and smarter living. Anchored in Uganda, Africa, this platform shows you how smallholder farmers can base on indigenous and scientific knowledge to transform themselves and eventually supply the world with safe products produced organically and sustainably. To quickly get you up to speed read about us, or the story that started everything.

BOS is about learning from the land, learning from each other, and using that knowledge to build more resilient and sustainable communities.

psychiatryAncestral Roots
scienceModern Science

Healthy Soil. Healthy Food. Healthy People. Healthy Planet.

This is the interconnected principle that guides our approaches. One thing to note: Humans are part of an integrated ecosystem. Everything matters.

compost

Feed the Soil

Nourishing the earth foundationally to ensure robust, nutrient-dense life cycles from the ground up.

recycling

Waste Nothing

Creating closed-loop systems where every byproduct becomes a resource for new growth.

eco

Climate-Smart

Adapting and building resilience through practices that actively draw down carbon and restore balance.

groups

Land Managers

Empowering local communities as the primary stewards and beneficiaries of their native ecosystems.

Our Core Initiatives

Pioneering Ecological Projects

Discover how we bridge ancestral agricultural wisdom with advanced scientific stewardship to heal soils, plant ecosystems, and empower self-sustaining communities.

Agroecology

Champion Farmer in Action

Welcome to my practical classroom this farm. This is where ideas meet reality.

Read Morearrow_forward
Soil Health

Feed the Soil. The Soil Feeds You.

There is an old adage: you cannot milk a cow without feeding it.

Read Morearrow_forward
Closed-Loop

Waste Nothing. Grow Everything.

What do you do with your leftover food? Your urine? Your faecal matter?

Read Morearrow_forward
Resilience

Climate-Smart Farming

Let’s be honest farming has become more unpredictable due to climate change. Rains are uncertain, and seasons are shifting.

Read Morearrow_forward
Stewardship

The Land Managers

Most people see themselves as landowners, farmers, or tenants. Their relationship with land is often centered on using it.

Read Morearrow_forward
Forestry

The Tree Billionaire Generation

This is one of the central ideas behind BOS a new, sustainable way of thinking about wealth.

Read Morearrow_forward
Social Economy

Smart and Sustainable Villages

Strong, self-reliant communities should be the true measure of sustainable development.

Read Morearrow_forward
Urban Agriculture

Urban Farming and Greener Cities

Many cities are nothing but rows of lifeless structures. But we are living beings we must breathe, not suffocate.

Read Morearrow_forward

Our Testimonials

What the Community is Saying

“

"BOS changed the way I see my land. I've tripled my harvest using indigenous soil practices. My children now have hope."

Sarah Namukasa

Community Farmer, Uganda

Blog Insights

Stay On Top of The
Latest From BOS

From ancient soil wisdom to cutting-edge urban farming, our blog covers the full spectrum of sustainable living. Learn, grow, and join a movement that is restoring the earth — one seed at a time.

15May

The Secret Life of Healthy Soil — What Science Says

Good soil is far more than dirt. Discover how billions of microorganisms beneath your feet are the true engine of food security and planetary health.

Read More
02Jun

How to Become a Tree Billionaire in 5 Years

Planting trees is not just an act of love for the environment — it is a generational investment. Learn how BOS is turning ordinary farmers into Tree Billionaires.

Read More
20Jun

Urban Rooftop Farming: Feeding Cities from Above

Cities need not be food deserts. Discover how BOS Smart Villages are transforming rooftops into productive organic gardens that feed families and build community.

Read More

About Us

Rooted in Tradition, Growing for the Future. The Bill of Senga is a sustainable living hub where indigenous knowledge meets modern agricultural science to build thriving ecosystems.
